LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 8TH, 9TH, 10TH AND 11TH CAUSES OF ACTION. PUBLIC EMPLOYMENT IS NOT HELD BY CONTRACT BUT BY STATUTE. (MILLER VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA (1977) 18 CAL.3D 808). THE DOCTRINE OF ESTOPPEL BY AGENCY MAY NOT BE APPLIED TO HOLD THE DEPARTMENT LIABLE. (BOREN VS. STATE PERSONNEL BOARD (1951) 37 CAL.2ND 634.) THE SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT IS DISMISSED AS IT IS NOT A PROPER PARTY IN THIS ACTION. (DARBY VS. PASADENA POLICE (5TH CIR. 1991) 939 F.2D 311.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Stacey A. Lucas, Esq. appearing for Defendant CCSF and moving party. (The Tentative Ruling is not being contested.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the 8th, 9th, 10th and 11th causes of action. Public employment is not held by contract but by statute. (Miller vs. State of California (1977) 18 Cal.3d 808). he doctrine of estoppel by agency may not be applied to hold the department liable. (Boren vs. State Personnel Board (1951) 37 Cal.2nd 634.) The San Francisco Police Department is dismissed as it is not a proper party in this action. (Darby vs. Pasadena Police (5th Cir. 1991) 939 F.2d 311.) Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
